step_by_step_thinking:
Numbness experienced during shaving following a parotidectomy suggests damage to a branch of the **auriculotemporal nerve**. Given that the parotid gland lies adjacent to structures innervated by branches of the auriculotemporal nerve, particularly those involved in sensation over the skin of the cheek and temporal area, any trauma or resection during the parotidectomy could lead to numbness along these distributions.
